description: Builds and advances technical solutions through curiosity, depth, and creation.

signals: Actively learns and applies team-defined tools, practices, and standards
Seeks guidance to follow existing implementations and patterns
examples: Built a small REST API by following an existing implementation and best practices
Asked a mentor to review initial changes to align with team conventions

signals: Becomes the go-to person for one technology, tool, or methodology
Expands expertise proactively beyond immediate tasks
examples: Documented OAuth authentication patterns for the team
Introduced a lint rule set to improve consistency

signals: Introduces new tools or practices that improve team effectiveness
examples: Piloted a feature flag library and rolled it out with a migration plan
Benchmarked alternative data stores and presented trade-offs

signals: Deeply understands the team’s tech stack and its operational characteristics
Guides teammates on architecture, testing, and reliability
examples: Created guidance on CI/CD best practices for the service suite
Led pairing sessions to improve testing strategies

signals: Masters the broader technical ecosystem and drives cross-team improvements
Shapes standards that raise quality and velocity
examples: Standardized observability patterns adopted by multiple teams
Refactored a shared library to simplify onboarding across services

signals: Designs or develops new technologies, tools, or practices with multi-team impact
Ensures new capabilities are widely adoptable and documented
examples: Built a contract-testing framework rolled out across several product areas
Created a migration toolkit that reduced upgrade time across teams

signals: Creates or steers technology that shifts how the organization delivers software
Sets long-term technical direction and mentors others to extend it
examples: Defined a new platform capability adopted org-wide (e.g., multi-tenant runtime)
Established a durable tech strategy with reference implementations and playbooks
